mcqs.question: What is searching in data structures?

options.text: Finding a specific element in a collection

mcqs.explanation: Searching is the process of finding a specific element or determining its presence within a collection of data.

mcqs.question: Which searching algorithm works on both sorted and unsorted data?
mcqs.explanation: Linear search works on both sorted and unsorted data because it checks each element sequentially without assuming any ordering.

mcqs.question: The best case time complexity of linear search occurs when:

options.text: The element is at the end
The element is at the first position
The array has only one element

mcqs.explanation: The best case for linear search is O(1), which happens when the target element is found at the very first position.

mcqs.question: Binary search divides the search space by:
mcqs.explanation: Binary search divides the search space in half with each comparison, which gives it O(log n) time complexity.

mcqs.question: If a sorted array has 1024 elements, binary search will find any element in at most how many comparisons?
mcqs.explanation: Binary search needs at most ceil(log2(1024)) + 1 = 11 comparisons. Since log2(1024) = 10, plus one more comparison gives 11.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of iterative linear search?
mcqs.explanation: Iterative linear search uses O(1) extra space as it only needs a loop variable and possibly a flag.

mcqs.question: The unsuccessful search in linear search has a time complexity of:
mcqs.explanation: An unsuccessful linear search must check every element in the collection before determining the target is not present, taking O(n) time.
